briefing:
# Atlantic Fintech Daily Briefing
### July 1, 2026 🎉 Happy Canada Day!

---

## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign payment system, challenging the dominance of Visa and Mastercard across the continent.

- **[Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cvgmv98ez3zo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — President Trump's crypto-related earnings have dramatically outpaced his income from real estate and branded merchandise in his first year back in office.

- **[Affirm now available across Bed Bath & Beyond's brands](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-wire-news-releases-pmn/affirm-now-available-across-bed-bath-beyonds-brands-giving-shoppers-more-flexibility-for-the-purchases-that-make-a-house-feel-like-home)** — *Financial Post* — Affirm has expanded its buy-now-pay-later embedded finance offering across Bed Bath & Beyond's family of brands, giving consumers more flexible payment options at checkout.

- **[Regnology to Acquire Fed Reporter, Accelerating U.S. Leadership and Advancing Regulatory Modernization](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-wire-news-releases-pmn/regnology-to-acquire-fed-reporter-accelerating-u-s-leadership-and-advancing-regulatory-modernization)** — *Financial Post* — Regnology's acquisition of Fed Reporter positions it as a central regulatory technology partner for over 4,000 U.S. financial institutions.

- **[Expensify's AI Expands to Expense Automation, Spend Insights, and Agents](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-wire-news-releases-pmn/expensifys-ai-expands-to-expense-automation-spend-insights-and-agents)** — *Financial Post* — Expensify has launched AI-powered expense automation, spend analytics, and workflow agents, enabling users to manage finances through natural language via email, text, or in-app.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[AI minister says Ottawa considering taking the lead on AI investment rounds](https://betakit.com/ai-minister-says-ottawa-considering-taking-the-lead-on-ai-investment-rounds/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government is exploring a role as a lead investor in AI funding rounds, signalling a more active stance in shaping Canada's technology investment landscape.

- **[Canada's economy reverses months of slow growth with 0.5% boost in April](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/april-gdp-numbers-canada-9.7253694?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's GDP grew 0.5% in April, reversing a prolonged period of sluggish economic performance and raising cautious optimism about the broader business environment.

- **[US finance app SoFi acquires Composer to add AI investing strategies](https://betakit.com/us-finance-app-sofi-acquires-composer-to-add-ai-investing-strategies/)** — *BetaKit* — SoFi has acquired AI-driven investing platform Composer to deepen its automated investment strategy offerings, a move with implications for Canadian digital wealth competitors.

- **[Changes are coming to auto insurance policies in Ontario. Here's what you need to know](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/toronto/ontario-auto-insurance-changes-9.7249103?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Ontario is implementing significant reforms to auto insurance policy structures, which could offer a preview of insurtech and embedded insurance shifts across other Canadian provinces.

- **[Regulator launches inquiry into contentious fees charged by Rogers, Bell and Telus](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/roger-bell-telus-fees-9.7254328?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian telecom regulator has opened an inquiry into disputed fees levied by the country's three major carriers, a development relevant to mobile payments and financial services bundled with telecom products.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — A useful evergreen reference as Canada's open banking framework continues to evolve — a reminder of how far (and how slowly) the journey has come, with direct relevance to Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs preparing for open banking participation.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis of how open banking reshapes payments competition, highly relevant for Atlantic Fintech stakeholders tracking how smaller regional players can leverage open data frameworks to compete.

- **[Vector Institute launches open-source AI bias detector](https://betakit.com/vector-institute-launches-open-source-ai-bias-detector/)** — *BetaKit* — Toronto's Vector Institute has released an open-source tool to detect AI bias, a critical resource for Atlantic Canadian fintechs building AI-driven credit, underwriting, or financial inclusion products.

---

*Curated by Atlantic Fintech | Building the fintech ecosystem across Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, PEI, and Newfoundland & Labrador.*
